description: Concrete tests trigger and workflow eval definitions through Milestone 4, including the must-run validation boundary pack and one pinned cryptoli real-repo workflow case.

trigger_evals.goal: Exercise tests' trigger boundary on behavior changes and stale executable truth.

cases.prompt: The parser now rejects empty names, but the existing unit tests still accept them. Sync the tests and run the smallest meaningful command.
cases.why: Changed behavior now needs executable-truth follow-through.

cases.prompt: Update AGENTS.md so the Commands table matches the current repo.
cases.why: Durable repo-truth sync belongs to specs.

cases.prompt: Review this test diff for missing edge cases and give me a verdict.

trigger_evals.goal: Protect tests' ownership of executable truth without absorbing planning or consultation.

cases.prompt: This repo has no automated coverage around the config loader, but execution work now depends on it. Add the smallest durable automated layer and report any blocked higher-layer gaps.
cases.why: Safe execution is blocked by missing executable truth, so tests owns the gap-close.

cases.prompt: Create a durable plan file for the next two testing milestones and keep it resumable.
cases.why: Durable task-state ownership belongs to plan.

cases.prompt: I do not know whether unit, integration, or smoke tests matter most here. Read the repo and recommend the safest next move.
cases.why: The decision is still unclear, so consult owns it.

workflow_evals.goal: Judge whether tests can sync an existing suite with changed behavior using the smallest applicable layer.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether the tests skill materially improves layer selection and coverage sync on an existing suite.

scenario.user_request: The parser now rejects empty names. Update the existing unit suite and run the smallest meaningful command.
repo_state: The repo already has unit tests under tests/unit.
No integration or e2e layer is required for this isolated parser change.
AGENTS.md says to separate unrelated baseline failures from problems introduced by the current work.

assertions: Identifies the applicable existing test layer before writing coverage.
Adds or updates tests for the changed behavior and the key edge case.
Runs a targeted command for the owning suite.
Reports unrelated baseline failures separately if they exist.
review_questions: Do the tests prove behavior instead of implementation details?
Does the update stay proportional instead of inventing new infrastructure?

workflow_evals.goal: Judge whether tests can bootstrap the minimum durable coverage on a weak repo without overstating what is now protected.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether the tests skill bootstraps the smallest credible automated layer without overstating coverage.

scenario.user_request: There is no automated coverage around the config loader, but execute work now depends on it. Add the smallest durable test layer.
repo_state: The repo has no current tests for the config loader.
AGENTS.md prefers the smallest credible automated layer over a speculative overhaul.
Higher layers may still be relevant later but are not yet wired.

assertions: Bootstraps a minimal durable automated layer instead of a full testing rewrite.
Uses the repo's existing structure and helpers when available.
States any blocked or still-uncovered higher layers explicitly.
review_questions: Does the result improve safety without implying more coverage than was actually added?

workflow_evals.goal: Judge whether tests can extend the smallest applicable executable-truth layer on the pinned cryptoli fixture while respecting its real backend/frontend/admin test topology.

optional.why: Use this only when skill-vs-no-skill output clarifies whether the tests skill improves layer selection and honest gap reporting on a real monorepo fixture.

scenario.user_request: A backend change landed in cryptoli without enough automated coverage. Add the smallest durable automated layer and report any still-blocked higher-layer gaps.
repo_state: The pinned fixture has backend unit tests in apps/backend/src/**/*.spec.ts, backend integration tests in apps/backend/test/integration, backend e2e tests in apps/backend/test/e2e, and separate Vitest suites for frontend and admin.
The evaluation branch under test adds backend behavior without the matching integration coverage.
AGENTS.md and specs/testing-strategy.md require applicable layers, not one blanket suite.

assertions: Discovers the actual backend, frontend, and admin test topology before choosing a layer.
Adds or updates the smallest applicable backend test layer for the changed behavior instead of inventing unrelated infrastructure.
Runs the targeted backend command for the owning layer and reports blocked higher layers honestly.
Does not broaden into repo-truth editing or unrelated frontend/admin test work.
review_questions: Does the result improve executable truth on the real repo without overstating what is now covered?
